slides.title: Eight layouts, one design language
slides.subtitle: Every pptx-deck layout demonstrated in one editable file.
slides.author: ZimaBlueAI · viz-deck mode 5

slides.title: Section divider
slides.subtitle: A chapter break — use 3 to 5 per deck so structure is visible at a glance

slides.title: Three to six points, with optional sub-notes
slides.lead: Use for the workhorse content slide — when you have a thesis sentence and 3–6 supporting points. Sub-notes shrink the cognitive load on each bullet.

bullets.text: Bullet text uses 22 pt body weight
bullets.note: sub-note in muted 16 pt under the bullet
more than 6 → split into two slides

bullets.text: Bullets should be sentences, not phrases
bullets.note: fragments force the reader to fill gaps

bullets.text: Lead line above is your one-sentence thesis
bullets.note: if you cut the bullets, the lead must still stand alone

slides.title: When you need an explicit compare-and-contrast

right.heading: Proposed
items: Same item count on both sides
Pair items by row — visual scan rhythm
Each row should be a real contrast
Color the accent bar only on one side

slides.title: Three to nine metric tiles in a responsive grid

slides.quote: A slide deck is the worst possible artifact — except for every other artifact stakeholders will actually open.
slides.author: Pulse industry truth
slides.role: Said by every CTO at every board meeting

slides.title: That's the full set
slides.subtitle: Eight layouts. One JSON spec format. Native DrawingML output.
